questions.question: Explain the concept of constructors in Python with reference to __new__ and __init__ methods. Discuss how default parameters can be used in constructors, their order of precedence, and when __new__ is preferred over __init__. Provide a code example demonstrating a class where you use default parameters in the constructor to initialize instance variables and explain the flow of object creation.
keyPoints: Explain __new__ vs __init__ and their execution order
Discuss default parameters in constructors with syntax
Show parameter precedence and how mutable default arguments cause issues
Demonstrate __new__ for singleton pattern or immutable types
Provide working code example with proper initialization

questions.question: Consider a scenario for a university management system where you have a base class 'Employee' with a method 'calculate_salary()'. Derive two classes 'Teacher' and 'AdministrativeStaff' from Employee. Override the calculate_salary() method in both derived classes to include different allowances. Write Python code to demonstrate inheritance, method overriding, and the use of super() to extend parent class functionality. Also explain how constructor inheritance works when the base class constructor requires parameters.
keyPoints: Define base class Employee with constructor and calculate_salary method
Create derived classes Teacher and AdministrativeStaff
Override calculate_salary with different allowance calculations
Use super() to call parent class methods and constructors
Demonstrate polymorphism with a function accepting Employee objects

questions.question: Differentiate between class methods, static methods, and instance methods in Python. Explain when and why you would use each type. Provide a code example of a class 'BankAccount' that uses: (a) instance method for deposit/withdrawal, (b) class method to get/set interest rate affecting all accounts, (c) static method to validate account number format. Discuss the @classmethod and @staticmethod decorators.
keyPoints: Differentiate instance methods, class methods, and static methods
Explain @classmethod and @staticmethod decorators with syntax
Show when to use each type with BankAccount example
Demonstrate class variable usage with class methods
Include static method for validation logic

questions.question: Write a Python program to implement a 'Student' class for a course registration system that meets the following requirements: (a) Use a constructor with default values for major='Computer Science' and year=1, (b) Include a class variable to track total number of students enrolled, (c) Implement a class method to display total enrollment count, (d) Implement a static method to validate student ID format (must be 8 alphanumeric characters), (e) Add a method to calculate GPA based on grades. Create multiple student objects and demonstrate all functionality.
keyPoints: Constructor with default parameters for major and year
Class variable for tracking total students
Class method using @classmethod decorator
Static method for ID validation
Method for GPA calculation with grades input
Demonstrate object creation and all method calls

questions.question: The following code has errors. Identify and fix all issues: 'class Book: def __init__(self, title, author=''): self.title = title self.author = author @staticmethod def get_format(title, price): return f"{title}: ${price}" @classmethod def create_from_dict(cls, data): return cls(data['title'], data['author']) b1 = Book("Python Basics") b2 = Book("Advanced Python", "John Doe") print(Book.get_format("Python", 500)) print(Book.create_from_dict({"title": "OOP", "author": "Smith"}))'. Explain what each corrected component does.
keyPoints: Identify missing colons and indentation issues
Fix the static method to be properly defined
Explain class method cls parameter usage
Demonstrate object creation with different constructors
Show static method call without instance
